title: Bridge Essay: TDCS to the right anterior temporal lobe facilitates insight problem-solving.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through TDCS, to, the, right, anterior.

**TDCS to the right anterior temporal lobe facilitates insight problem-solving.** — Sci Rep. PMID: 31969588. DOI: 10.1038/s41598-020-57724-1. Problem-solving is essential for advances in cultural, social, and scientific knowledge. It is also one of the most challenging cognitive processes to facilitate. Some problem-solving is deliberate, but frequently people solve problems with asudden insight, also known as a Eureka or "Aha!" moment. The advantage of solving problems via insight is that these solutions are more accurate, relying on aunique pattern of neural activity, compared to deliberative strategies.
